title: Fungal Isolation and Cell DNA Extraction from Soil Samples
author: Nuttapon Pombubpa

steps.title: Fungal Identification
steps.instruction: Fungal Identification

steps.title: Sample Preparation
steps.instruction: Sample Preparation

steps.title: Weigh 1 g of soil sample
steps.instruction: Weigh 1 g of soil sample.

steps.title: Cultivation Using Spread Plate Method
steps.instruction: Cultivation Using Spread Plate Method

steps.title: Perform serial dilutions of soil using...
steps.instruction: Perform serial dilutions of soil using sterile water (10⁻³ to 10⁻⁶ dilutions with 9 mL sterile water).

steps.title: Colony Purification
steps.instruction: Colony Purification

steps.title: Spread 100 µL of each dilution...
steps.instruction: Spread 100 µL of each dilution onto culture media (PDA and MEA) that contains antibacteria (tetracyclin 40 mg/L).

steps.title: Incubate plates at room temperature (25-30...
steps.instruction: Incubate plates at room temperature (25-30 ºC) for 5–7 days until fungal colonies are visible.

steps.title: Transfer fungal hyphae from mixed colonies...
steps.instruction: Transfer fungal hyphae from mixed colonies to fresh PDA plates for purification.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (25-30 ºC)...
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (25-30 ºC) for 5–7 days to obtain pure colonies. * Note: Repeat this step until a single culture is obtained.

steps.title: Colony morphology and microscopic documentation The...
steps.instruction: Colony morphology and microscopic documentation The colony features based on: Color (top and reverse) Margin (smooth/entire, filamentous, lobed, or irregular) Elevation (flat, raised, umbonate (with a central bump), or wrinkled) Texture (powdery, velvety, cottony, granular, glabrous, or creamy) Zonation (presence or not) Radial furrow (presence or not) Exudate drop (presence or not) The microscopic can be prepared by slide culture technique Prepare slide cultures to observe substrate and aerial hyphal structures. Adding Lactophenol-Cotton Blue stain to the growth colony is recommended for better visualization. Characterize the cell structure based on: Hyphae (septate or aseptate) Spore - Asexual (consisting of macroconidium, microconidium, chlamydospore, arthrospore, blastospore, or sporangiospore) Spore - Sexual (type of ascospore, basidiospore, or zygospore) Sporangiophore (consisting of aporangium, columella, or hyphae-stalk) Conidiophore (type of simple, complex, acrotheca, cladosporium or phialophora) * Read the technique of slide culture here: https://sea-armi.github.io/research/SlideCulture

steps.title: Fungal identity confirmation Compare the colony...
steps.instruction: Fungal identity confirmation Compare the colony and slide culture results with fungal book determination. Reference: Barnett & Hunter, 1998. Illustrated Genera of Imperfect Fungi, Fourth Edition SEA-ARMi, 2025. Filamentous WHO FPPL target: Mucorales, Eumycetoma causative agents, and Fusarium group *Read the Barnett & Hunter, 1998. Illustrated Genera of Imperfect Fungi, Fourth Edition at the attached file *Read SEA-ARMi, 2025. targeted filamentous WHO-FPPL here: https://sea-armi.github.io/research/TargetedWHO

steps.title: The pure identified colony can be...
steps.instruction: The pure identified colony can be used for the cDNA extraction.

steps.title: Use the AllPrep Fungal DNA Kit...
steps.instruction: Use the AllPrep Fungal DNA Kit to extract DNA from the cell hyphae.

steps.title: Prepare fungal cell samples and resuspend in Solution HC
steps.instruction: Prepare fungal cell samples and resuspend in Solution HC.

steps.title: Perform cell lysis with vortexing
steps.instruction: Perform cell lysis with vortexing.

steps.title: Load lysate into MB Spin Columns
steps.instruction: Load lysate into MB Spin Columns.

steps.title: Wash columns with Solutions RA and RW
steps.instruction: Wash columns with Solutions RA and RW.

steps.title: Elute DNA with Solution EB
steps.instruction: Elute DNA with Solution EB.

steps.title: Submission of Isolated DNA
steps.instruction: Submission of Isolated DNA

steps.title: DNA Qualification and Quantification
steps.instruction: DNA Qualification and Quantification

steps.title: Perform agarose gel electrophoresis to verify DNA quality and purity
steps.instruction: Perform agarose gel electrophoresis to verify DNA quality and purity.

steps.title: Use a Nanodrop spectrophotometer to quantify...
steps.instruction: Use a Nanodrop spectrophotometer to quantify DNA concentration and purity (at least 10 ng/µL).

steps.title: Send the purified fungal DNA to...
steps.instruction: Send the purified fungal DNA to the FAILSAFE Project Team, SEA-ARMi at Chulalongkorn University for further analysis.

steps.title: Molecular identification For molecular identification, single...
steps.instruction: Molecular identification For molecular identification, single fungal colony DNA extraction follows Step 7 (cDNA extraction) . The fungal genome is amplified using the Internal Transcribed Spacer (ITS) region, with the following primers: ITS1 forward primer: 5' TCCGTAGGTGAACCTGCGG 3' ITS4 reverse primer: 5' TCCTCCGCTTATTGATATGC 3' These primers are used to amplify conserved regions of fungal DNA (White et al., 1990). PCR Mixture (25 µL total volume) : 12.5 µL PCR master mix with Taq DNA polymerase 0.5 µL forward primer 0.5 µL reverse primer 12 µL solution (DNA template diluted with molecular-grade water, ensuring a final DNA concentration of 100 ng) The negative control is PCR mixture without DNA template and the positive control is identified fungal. White, T. J., Bruns, T., Lee, S. J. W. T., & Taylor, J.. Amplification and direct sequencing of fungal ribosomal RNA genes for phylogenetics. PCR protocols: a guide to methods and applications. https://www.researchgate.net/publication/223397588_White_T_J_T_D_Bruns_S_B_Lee_and_J_W_Taylor_Amplification_and_direct_sequencin

steps.title: The PCR conditions for amplifying ITS were as follows
steps.instruction: The PCR conditions for amplifying ITS were as follows: Initial denaturation step at 95°C for 5 minutes 25 cycles of 1 minute at 95°C 25 seconds at 56°C 30 seconds at 72°C with a final extension at 72°C for 5 minutes Holding the PCR product at 4°C.

steps.title: The PCR product is analyzed using 1
steps.instruction: The PCR product is analyzed using 1.5% agarose gel electrophoresis, with a 1 Kb DNA ladder as a size marker. Electrophoresis is performed at 100V for 22 minutes in 1× TBE buffer.

steps.title: High-quality PCR products will be sequenced...
steps.instruction: High-quality PCR products will be sequenced using Sanger sequencing, and the resulting sequence files will be identified using BLAST (NCBI database).

steps.title: Inoculate the pure fungal colony to...
steps.instruction: Inoculate the pure fungal colony to PDB and incubate for 5 days to get enough hyphae at room temperature (25-30 ºC) with 100 rpm.
